extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE Alejandro timed his sprint perfectly from Sagan's wheel after final kilometer tight corners, Garcia Cortina fading after opening too early.
tactical_patterns: Movistar let sprinter teams chase 12-minute break with Machado at +22min GC, preserving energy while gaining positioning intelligence.
Valverde concealed himself behind Sagan's green jersey into final corner, exploiting visibility blind spot when Garcia Cortina opened prematurely.
Break given 12min with Machado at +22min shows peloton calculates chase effort based on GC threat, not time gap alone.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: second stage win moves him to 37sec from red, demonstrating sprint speed competitive with specialists despite GC focus
rider_signals.GARCIA CORTINA Ivan: negative: opened sprint too early before final corner despite local knowledge advantage, poor timing judgment cost certain victory
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: mechanical issues mid-race but led final corner, suggesting form adequate but not dominant against GC riders sprinting
rider_signals.MACHADO Tiago: positive: Katyusha allowed solo survival to 6km despite 12min gap, extracting maximum breakaway value before inevitable catch
